introduction:
House and Land Documents typically refer to Property Registration, which is a mandatory process governed by the Registration Act, 1908. Registering your property ensures legal ownership and protects against disputes in Telangana.

The registration process involves paying stamp duty and registration fees, and must be completed at the local Sub-Registrar's Office within 4 months of the execution of the sale deed. It serves as an undeniable record of title and rights over the property.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Property registration is mandatory under Section 17 of the Registration Act.
  • Stamp duty charges vary by property type and gender (often discounted for women in Telangana).
  • Registration must be done within 4 months of executing the document.
  • Aadhaar authentication and biometric verification of buyer, seller, and witnesses are strictly required.
  • Encumbrance Certificate (EC) should be checked prior to purchase to ensure title is clear.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Property Registration

Note: Always keep the original documents and two sets of self-attested photocopies ready before visiting the SRO.
Identity & Address Proof
  • Aadhaar Card of Buyer and Seller (Mandatory)
  • PAN Card (Mandatory for transactions over ₹50,000)
  • Passport / Voter ID (Optional supplementary ID)
Property Documents
  • Original Drafted Sale Deed / Conveyance Deed
  • Previous title deeds (Chain of Documents)
  • Encumbrance Certificate (EC)
  • Latest Property Tax Receipts
  • Sanctioned Building Plan (if applicable)
Other Requirements
  • Two Passport-size photos of all parties
  • Payment receipt of Stamp Duty and Registration Fee (e-Challan)
  • TDS payment receipt (if property value > ₹50 Lakhs)
Witnesses
  • Two independent witnesses
  • Witness ID Proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID)
  • Passport-size photos of witnesses
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Online Pre-Registration & Data Entry

Telangana offers an online Public Data Entry facility via the official portal to simplify the physical registration process.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Official Portal
    Go to registration.telangana.gov.in
  2. Calculate Stamp Duty
    Use the online calculator to determine the exact stamp duty and registration fee for your property.
  3. Pay Fees
    Pay the calculated duties online via the state receipt portal and generate the e-Challan.
  4. Data Entry
    Fill in the property details, buyer/seller information, and witness details online to generate a token number.
  5. Book Appointment
    Use the portal to book an appointment slot at your local Sub-Registrar Office (SRO). Print the acknowledgment slip.
Tip: Always double-check property boundaries and survey numbers during Data Entry.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Physical Registration at SRO

The final step of property registration must be completed physically at the Sub-Registrar Office.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Arrive at the SRO
    Visit the Sub-Registrar Office 15 minutes before your scheduled appointment time with the buyer, seller, and two witnesses.
  2. Submit Documents
    Present the appointment slip, online token number, original Draft Deed, e-Challan receipt, and all original ID proofs to the officer.
  3. Scrutiny & Biometrics
    The Sub-Registrar will verify the documents. Biometrics (fingerprints) and photographs of all parties will be captured on the spot.
  4. Signatures
    All parties and witnesses must sign the registry records in front of the Sub-Registrar.
  5. Collect Receipt
    Collect the registration receipt. You will be notified when the officially registered deed is ready for collection (usually within a few days).
afterApplying:
After Registration — What Next?

Property Mutation

Registering the deed does not automatically update municipal or land revenue records. Apply for property mutation at the local Tehsil/Panchayat.

Update Utility Bills

Apply to transfer electricity, water connections, and property tax records to your name using the newly registered deed and mutation certificate.

Digital Copy Download

Download the digitally signed registered document online from the official portal (if available in your district) using your document number.

Safekeeping

Store the original registered deed in a highly secure place, such as a bank locker, as it is the absolute proof of your ownership.
